verdict_reasoning: EREG is an EGF-family ligand synthesized as a single-pass TM proligand (proEREG) that is cleaved by ADAM sheddases to release the soluble active EGFR/ERBB4 ligand. The TM precursor is transiently present on the cell surface and can mediate juxtacrine signaling, but the dominant biological actor is the shed soluble form. This fits dual_localization: real but transient TM stage with a dominant soluble pool.
